skill.name: writing-shape
skill.description: Guides a conversational writing session that turns markdown notes, fragments, or rough drafts into a structured article.
skill.summary: Shape raw markdown material into a publishable article through guided drafting and revision.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance alerts were evaluated as false positives from Markdown syntax and prose. The skill does request user-directed file reads and article writes, so the remaining risk is limited filesystem access for draft creation. No prompt injection, network access, secrets handling, or executable payload was found.

low_findings.title: User-Directed File Read and Write Workflow
low_findings.description: The skill asks the assistant to read the raw markdown file and append agreed content to a separate article file. This is expected for the writing workflow, but it still requires normal care with user-provided paths and existing edits.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file read and write behavior is directly described in the skill instructions. The risk is low because the operations are user-directed and there is no evidence of hidden paths or destructive writes.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution twice at line 49. That line only uses Markdown inline code to show callout syntax, and it does not instruct execution of shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 49 contains Markdown formatting examples inside prose. There is no executable code, command runner, or user input passed to a shell.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged weak cryptography patterns at lines 3, 24, and 49. These lines contain article-writing instructions and Markdown examples, with no cryptographic algorithm or security primitive present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are plain natural language and Markdown syntax. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or related weak cryptographic use.

low_findings.title: Static System Reconnaissance Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged system reconnaissance at line 12. The line asks where to save the article and says to re-read the article before writing, not to enumerate system information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited instruction is about preserving user edits in a writing document. It does not request environment details, host data, directory discovery, or credential lookup.

content.value_statement: Raw notes often lack a clear argument and readable order. This skill guides a focused session that turns markdown material into a coherent article.
actual_capabilities: Reads a markdown source file as raw material before drafting.
Creates two or three candidate openings with different article angles.
Builds the article paragraph by paragraph through user approval.
Compares prose, lists, tables, callouts, quotes, and code blocks for each section.
Appends agreed article content to a separate draft file.
Re-reads the draft before writing so user edits are preserved.
limitations: It depends on a markdown input file supplied by the user.
It does not research new material outside the supplied notes.
It does not publish the article or optimize for a platform by default.
It requires active user choices during the shaping session.

use_cases.title: Turn Research Notes Into a Draft
use_cases.description: Convert a collection of markdown notes into a clear article with an opening, sequence, and supporting sections.

use_cases.title: Shape Technical Fragments Into a Post
use_cases.description: Organize examples, observations, and rough explanations into a readable technical article.

use_cases.title: Improve an Existing Rough Draft
use_cases.description: Use the source material to rebuild weak transitions, remove drift, and select better formats for each idea.

prompt_templates.title: Start From Notes
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a simple article draft.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the writing-shape skill with my markdown notes at [path]. Ask where to save the article, then propose candidate openings.

prompt_templates.title: Choose an Article Angle
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for selecting a stronger thesis.
prompt_templates.prompt: Read my raw material at [path]. Draft three openings with different arguments, then help me choose the best direction.

prompt_templates.title: Build Section by Section
prompt_templates.prompt: Use [source path] as the raw material and [draft path] as the article. Add one agreed paragraph or block at a time.

prompt_templates.title: Challenge Structure and Format
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for a rigorous editorial session.
prompt_templates.prompt: Shape [source path] into [draft path]. Push back on weak transitions and argue whether each beat needs prose, a list, a table, a callout, or a quote.

output_examples.input: A markdown file contains notes, partial arguments, and examples for a long-form article.
output_examples.output: The assistant proposes several openings, helps select one, then builds the article in approved sections.

output_examples.input: A rough draft has unclear transitions and several ideas that feel out of order.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies the article promise, reorders the next beats, and rewrites only the agreed parts.

output_examples.input: A section contains repeated comparisons that may be prose, a list, or a table.
output_examples.output: The assistant explains the format tradeoffs and drafts the selected structure in the article file.

best_practices: Provide a clear markdown source file before starting the session.
Choose or combine one opening before drafting the body.
Review each paragraph or block before it is appended to the article.
anti_patterns: Do not expect the skill to invent missing research or examples.
Do not use it for unattended bulk rewriting of many files.
Do not skip the opening choice, because it anchors the article structure.

faq.question: What kind of input does this skill need?
faq.answer: It works best with a markdown file that contains notes, fragments, transcripts, or a rough draft.

faq.question: Will it edit my raw material file?
faq.answer: No. The skill treats the source material as read-only and creates a separate article document.

faq.question: Can it write the full article at once?
faq.answer: The intended workflow is paragraph by paragraph, with user approval before each addition.

faq.question: Does it add new research?
faq.answer: No. It uses the supplied material and asks for missing examples when the article needs them.

faq.question: Can it choose between prose and structured formats?
faq.answer: Yes. It explicitly weighs prose, lists, tables, callouts, quotes, and code blocks.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
